mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 200k persons signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when PAYEMS growth momentum is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 141183k as of April 2015 reflects post-crisis recovery momentum
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with accelerating labor market tightening
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near zero (below 0.25%) while inflation measured by CPIAUCSL shows no acceleration, the real interest rate is deeply negative, signaling monetary accommodation that reduces equity risk premiums and supports equity valuations. This mechanism activates only when both conditions hold simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.11% and CPIAUCSL at 235.186 as of April 2015
regime_hint: post-crisis monetary accommodation with subdued inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (FEDFUNDS × CPIAUCSL growth) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A threshold breach where housing starts (HOUST) fall below 850k units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand contraction, which predicts negative equity returns as investors reprrice growth expectations downward. This threshold-triggered mechanism activates only when HOUST crosses below the 850k level.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 850k units in any month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 897k as of February 2015, above trigger threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with housing market vulnerability
failure_condition: HOUST breaches below 850k but SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 1.5% annualized rate while remaining positive, signaling a supply-side slowdown that compresses profit margins and equity valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts negative returns when INDPRO momentum weakens, reflecting deteriorating operational efficiency across the manufacturing base.
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration fails to predict negative 5d returns in > 52% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) move in tandem above their 6-month moving averages, signaling synchronized consumer and residential demand strength. This interaction-driven mechanism requires both series to confirm demand expansion simultaneously, predicting positive equity returns over 20 trading days when both conditions hold.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 441363M and HOUST at 897k as of early 2015
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion in late-cycle recovery
failure_condition: Both RSAFS and HOUST above their 6-month MAs but SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 55% of instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, signaling an abrupt deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers equity repricing as investors reduce growth expectations. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp within any 3-month rolling window
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months but SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized while the federal funds rate remains below 0.5%, creating a regime where monetary policy is accommodative relative to growth momentum. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive equity returns over 60 trading days as investors expect sustained growth without imminent policy tightening.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy during above-trend growth
failure_condition: Both conditions met but SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 55% of instances, or the interaction term shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both decelerate simultaneously (month-over-month growth rates fall below their 3-month averages), signaling synchronized weakness in production capacity and labor utilization. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth < 3-month MA AND PAYEMS MoM growth < 3-month MA
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.2161 and PAYEMS at 141183k as of March 2015
failure_condition: Both INDPRO and PAYEMS decelerate below their 3-month MAs but SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 58% of instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.
